contentBlocks.original:
श्रीवेङ्कटाद्रिनिलयः कमलाकामुकः पुमान् ।
अभङ्गुर विभूतिर्नस्तरङ्गयतु मङ्गलम्
श्रीमदखिल महीमण्डल मण्डन धरणिधर मण्डलाखण्डलस्य ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīveṅkaṭādrinilayaḥ kamalākāmukaḥ pumān .
abhaṅgura vibhūtirnastaraṅgayatu maṅgalam
śrīmadakhila mahīmaṇḍala maṇḍana dharaṇidhara maṇḍalākhaṇḍalasya .. 1 ..
contentBlocks.translation: May Sri Venkatesha, who dwells on the Venkata hill, the lover of Kamala (Lakshmi), the Purusha of inexhaustible glory — bestow auspiciousness upon us. (Salutations to the Lord) of the glorious undivided realm that adorns the entire circle of the earth, the axis of the sphere of worlds.

contentBlocks.original: निखिल सुरासुर वन्दित वराह क्षेत्र विभूषणस्य ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: nikhila surāsura vandita varāha kṣetra vibhūṣaṇasya .. 2 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Of Varaha Kshetra, honoured by all gods and demons.

contentBlocks.original:
शेषाचल गरुडाचल वृषभाचल नारायणाचलाञ्जनाचलादि शिखरि
मालाकुलस्य ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śeṣācala garuḍācala vṛṣabhācala nārāyaṇācalāñjanācalādi śikhari
mālākulasya .. 3 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Who dwells among the peaks of Sheshachala, Garudachala, Vrishabhachala, Narayanachala, Anjanachala and other hills.

contentBlocks.original:
नाथमुख बोधनिधि वीथिगुण साभरण सत्त्वनिधि तत्त्वनिधि
भक्तिगुणपूर्ण श्रीशैल पूर्ण गुणवशंवद परमपुरुष कृपापूर
विभ्रमदतुङ्गश‍ृङ्ग गलद्गगन गङ्गासमालिङ्गितस्य ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nāthamukha bodhanidhi vīthiguṇa sābharaṇa sattvanidhi tattvanidhi
bhaktiguṇapūrṇa śrīśaila pūrṇa guṇavaśaṃvada paramapuruṣa kṛpāpūra
vibhramadatuṅgaśṛṅga galadgagana gaṅgāsamāliṅgitasya .. 4 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Whose fame is spread by the great Acharya Nathamuni; adorned by the full Srishaila mountain; whose majestic peak is embraced by the flowing celestial Ganga streaming from the sky.

contentBlocks.original:
सीमातिगगुण रामानुज मुनि नामाङ्कित बहुभूमाश्रय सुरधामा लयवनरामा
यतवनसीमा परिवृत विशङ्कटतट निरन्तर विजृम्भित भक्तिरस
निर्झरानन्तार्याहार्य प्रस्रवण धारापूर विभ्रमद सलिलभर भरित
महा तटाकमण्डितस्य ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sīmātigaguṇa rāmānuja muni nāmāṅkita bahubhūmāśraya suradhāmā layavanarāmā
yatavanasīmā parivṛta viśaṅkaṭataṭa nirantara vijṛmbhita bhaktirasa
nirjharānantāryāhārya prasravaṇa dhārāpūra vibhramada salilabhara bharita
mahā taṭākamaṇḍitasya .. 5 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Whose hill is marked by Ramanuja's name, the great asylum for countless souls; whose sacred forests and borders are a home for the divine; around whose steep slopes flows the continuous spring of devotion, filling the great sacred tanks.

contentBlocks.original:
कलिकर्दममलमर्दन कलितोद्यम विलसद्यमनियमादिम मुनिगण निषेव्यमाण
प्रत्यक्षीभवन्निजसलिल समज्जननमज्जन निखिल पापनाशन पापनाशन
तिर्थाध्यासितस्य ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalikardamamalamardana kalitodyama vilasadyamaniyamādima munigaṇa niṣevyamāṇa
pratyakṣībhavannijasalila samajjananamajjana nikhila pāpanāśana pāpanāśana
tirthādhyāsitasya .. 6 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Where ascetics practicing yama and niyama serve the Lord; whose sacred waters — flowing visibly — destroy all sins of those who bathe in them; the sacred Papanashana tirtha.

contentBlocks.original:
मुरारि सेवक जराधिपीडित निरार्तिजीवन निराशभूसुर वरातिसुन्दर
सुराङ्गना रतिकराङ्ग सौष्ठव कुमारताकृति कुमारतारक
समापनोदय दमानपातक महापदामय विहापनोदित सकल भुवन विदित
कुमारधाराभिधान तिर्थाधिष्ठितस्य ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
murāri sevaka jarādhipīḍita nirārtijīvana nirāśabhūsura varātisundara
surāṅganā ratikarāṅga sauṣṭhava kumāratākṛti kumāratāraka
samāpanodaya damānapātaka mahāpadāmaya vihāpanodita sakala bhuvana vidita
kumāradhārābhidhāna tirthādhiṣṭhitasya .. 7 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Where the aged devotees of Murari (Vishnu) are healed of infirmities; whose beauty surpasses that of divine maidens; the Kumaradhara tirtha, famed in all worlds for washing away great sins and afflictions.

contentBlocks.original:
धरणि तलगत सकलहत कलिलशुभ सलिलगत बहुलविविध मलहति
चतुर रुचिरतर विलोकमात्रविदलित विविध महापातक स्वामिपुष्करिणी
समेतस्य ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dharaṇi talagata sakalahata kalilaśubha salilagata bahulavividha malahati
catura ruciratara vilokamātravidalita vividha mahāpātaka svāmipuṣkariṇī
sametasya .. 8 ..
contentBlocks.translation: The holy Svami Pushkarini, which destroys with a single glance all the great sins of those who immerse themselves in it.

contentBlocks.original:
बहुसङ्कट नरकावट पतदुत्कट कलिकङ्कट कलुषोद्भट जनपातक
विनिपातक रुचिनाटक करहाटक कलशाहृत कमलारत शुभमज्जन
जलसज्जन भरित निजदुरित हतिनिरत जनसतत निरर्गलपेयीयमान
सलिल सम्भृत विशङ्कट कटाहतीर्थ भूषितस्य ॥ ९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bahusaṅkaṭa narakāvaṭa patadutkaṭa kalikaṅkaṭa kaluṣodbhaṭa janapātaka
vinipātaka rucināṭaka karahāṭaka kalaśāhṛta kamalārata śubhamajjana
jalasajjana bharita nijadurita hatinirata janasatata nirargalapeyīyamāna
salila sambhṛta viśaṅkaṭa kaṭāhatīrtha bhūṣitasya .. 9 ..
contentBlocks.translation: The vast Katahatirtha — filled with pure, ever-flowing water, which those devoted to removing their sins drink freely, of the most beautiful and gleaming appearance.

contentBlocks.original:
एवमादिम भुरिमञ्जिम सर्वपातक गर्वहापक सिन्धुडम्बर
हारिशम्बर विविधविपुल पुण्यतीर्थ निवह निवासस्य ॥ १० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
evamādima bhurimañjima sarvapātaka garvahāpaka sindhuḍambara
hāriśambara vividhavipula puṇyatīrtha nivaha nivāsasya .. 10 ..
contentBlocks.translation: And thus this great sacred hill is adorned by many magnificent and sacred tirthas that wash away all pride of sin, most wonderful among oceans of purity.

contentBlocks.original: श्री वेङ्कटाचलस्य ॥ ११ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: śrī veṅkaṭācalasya .. 11 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Of Sri Venkatachala.

contentBlocks.original: शिखरशेखर महाकल्प शाखी ॥ १२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: śikharaśekhara mahākalpa śākhī .. 12 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Who is the great Kalpa tree with peaks as its branches.

contentBlocks.original:
खर्वीभवदतिगर्वि कृतगुरुमेर्विश
गिरिमुखोर्वीधरकुलदर्वीकर दयितोर्वी धरशिखरोर्वी सतत
सदुर्वी कृतिचणनवघनगर्वचर्वणनिपुण तनुकिरण मसृणित
गिरिशिखरशेखर तरुनिकर तिमिरः ॥ १३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kharvībhavadatigarvi kṛtagurumerviśa
girimukhorvīdharakuladarvīkara dayitorvī dharaśikharorvī satata
sadurvī kṛticaṇanavaghanagarvacarvaṇanipuṇa tanukiraṇa masṛṇita
giriśikharaśekhara tarunikara timiraḥ .. 13 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Whose gentle rays soothe the peaks of the great Meru — the snake-bearing mountains, the mountain chiefs; dispelling the pride of the heavy rain-clouds with his tender, smoothening golden beams.

contentBlocks.original:
वाणीपति शर्वाणीदयितेन्द्राणीश्वरमुखनाणीयो रसवेणी निभ शुभवाणी
नुतमहिमाणी यस्तरकोणी भवदखिलभुवन भवनोदरः ॥ १४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāṇīpati śarvāṇīdayitendrāṇīśvaramukhanāṇīyo rasaveṇī nibha śubhavāṇī
nutamahimāṇī yastarakoṇī bhavadakhilabhuvana bhavanodaraḥ .. 14 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Who is praised with auspicious speech by the lord Brahma, Shiva, Indra and their consorts — his glorious form is the innermost of the universe, whose cosmic body is all the worlds.

contentBlocks.original:
वैमानिक गुरुभूमाधिक गुण रामानुज कृतधामाकर करधामारिदर
ललामाच्छ कनक दामायित निजरामालयनवकिसलयमय तोरण
मालायितवनमालाधरः ॥ १५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaimānika gurubhūmādhika guṇa rāmānuja kṛtadhāmākara karadhāmāridara
lalāmāccha kanaka dāmāyita nijarāmālayanavakisalayamaya toraṇa
mālāyitavanamālādharaḥ .. 15 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Honoured by Ramanuja above all the great Acharyas, wearing the divine Tulasi garland, adorned with fresh shoots of the lotus — the wearer of the forest-garland (Vana-mala).

contentBlocks.original:
कालाम्बुद मालानिभ नीलालक जालावृत बालाब्ज सलीलामलफालाङ्क
समूलामृत धाराद्वयावधीरण धीरललिततर विशदतर घन
घनसारमयोर्ध्वपुण्ड्र रेखाद्वयरुचिरः ॥ १६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kālāmbuda mālānibha nīlālaka jālāvṛta bālābja salīlāmalaphālāṅka
samūlāmṛta dhārādvayāvadhīraṇa dhīralalitatara viśadatara ghana
ghanasāramayordhvapuṇḍra rekhādvayaruciraḥ .. 16 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Whose dark locks of hair are like rain-clouds; whose pure forehead is adorned with two vertical lines of white sandal like streams of divine nectar — the brilliant Urdhvapundra marks.

contentBlocks.original:
सुविकस्वरदलभास्वरकमलोदरगतमेदुरनवकेसरततिभासुरपरिपिञ्जर-
कनकाम्बरकलितादरललितोदरतदालम्बजम्भरिपुमणिस्तम्भ-
गम्भीरिमदम्भस्तम्भन समुज्जृम्भमाणपीवरोरुयुगल-
तदालम्बपृथुलकदलीमुकुलमदहरणजङ्घालजङ्घायुगलः ॥ १७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suvikasvaradalabhāsvarakamalodaragatameduranavakesaratatibhāsuraparipiñjara-
kanakāmbarakalitādaralalitodaratadālambajambharipumaṇistambha-
gambhīrimadambhastambhana samujjṛmbhamāṇapīvaroruyugala-
tadālambapṛthulakadalīmukulamadaharaṇajaṅghālajaṅghāyugalaḥ .. 17 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Whose large eyes are like a pair of lotus flowers; whose nose is like the beak of a parrot; whose cheeks are like mirrors of beauty; whose lips are red as coral.

contentBlocks.original:
नव्यदल भव्यकल पीतमल शोणिमल सन्मृदुल सत्किसलयाश्रुजलकारि
बलशोणतल पत्कमलनिजाश्रय बलबन्दीकृत शरदिन्दुमण्डली
विभ्रमदादभ्रशुभ्र पुनर्भवाधिष्ठिताङ्गुली
गाढनिपीडित पद्मासनः ॥ १८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
navyadala bhavyakala pītamala śoṇimala sanmṛdula satkisalayāśrujalakāri
balaśoṇatala patkamalanijāśraya balabandīkṛta śaradindumaṇḍalī
vibhramadādabhraśubhra punarbhavādhiṣṭhitāṅgulī
gāḍhanipīḍita padmāsanaḥ .. 18 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Whose beautiful neck is like the conch — the three rings of glory upon it; whose shoulders are strong and broad like the lion; whose sacred chest bears the mark of Srivatsa.

contentBlocks.original:
जानुतलावधिलम्भिविडम्बित वारणशुण्डा दण्डविजृम्भित नीलमणिमय
कल्पकशाखा विभ्रमदायि मृणाललतायत समुज्ज्वलतर कनक
वलयवेल्लितैकतर बाहुदण्डयुगलः ॥ १९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jānutalāvadhilambhiviḍambita vāraṇaśuṇḍā daṇḍavijṛmbhita nīlamaṇimaya
kalpakaśākhā vibhramadāyi mṛṇālalatāyata samujjvalatara kanaka
valayavellitaikatara bāhudaṇḍayugalaḥ .. 19 ..
contentBlocks.translation: On whose broad chest eternally dwells Mahalakshmi of the lotus face — who is the very embodiment of all virtues.

contentBlocks.original:
युगपदुदित कोटिखरकर हिमकर मण्डल जाज्ज्वल्यमान सुदर्शन
पाञ्चजन्य समुत्तुङ्गित श‍ृङ्गापरबाहुयुगलः ॥ २० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yugapadudita koṭikharakara himakara maṇḍala jājjvalyamāna sudarśana
pāñcajanya samuttuṅgita śṛṅgāparabāhuyugalaḥ .. 20 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Who wears the Kaustubha gem of blazing brilliance; adorned with the Vaijayanti garland; who bears on his four arms the conch Panchajanya, the disc Sudarshana, the mace Kaumodaki and the lotus.

contentBlocks.original:
अभिनवशाणा समुत्तेजित महामहानीलखण्ड मदखण्डननिपुण नवीन
परितप्त कार्तस्वरकवचित महनीय पृथुलसालग्राम परम्परागुम्फित
नाभिमण्डलपर्यन्त लम्बमानप्रालम्बदीप्ति
समालम्बित विशालवक्षस्थलः ॥ २१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
abhinavaśāṇā samuttejita mahāmahānīlakhaṇḍa madakhaṇḍananipuṇa navīna
paritapta kārtasvarakavacita mahanīya pṛthulasālagrāma paramparāgumphita
nābhimaṇḍalaparyanta lambamānaprālambadīpti
samālambita viśālavakṣasthalaḥ .. 21 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Whose waist is adorned with the golden Pitambara (yellow silk), tied with a jeweled belt; whose thighs and calves are like the trunk of a young elephant; whose feet with their beautiful toe-nails are resplendent like a row of full moons.

contentBlocks.original:
गङ्गाझरतुङ्गाकृतिभङ्गावलिभङ्गावह सौधावलि बावह धारानिभ
हारावलि दूराहत गेहान्तरमोहावह महिम मसृणित महातिमिरः ॥ २२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gaṅgājharatuṅgākṛtibhaṅgāvalibhaṅgāvaha saudhāvali bāvaha dhārānibha
hārāvali dūrāhata gehāntaramohāvaha mahima masṛṇita mahātimiraḥ .. 22 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Who is the supreme Brahman — of infinite knowledge, power, strength, dominion, valor and splendour; without beginning, middle or end; of infinite, undecaying and unchanging glory.

contentBlocks.original:
पिङ्गाकृतिभृङ्गारुनिभाङ्गार दलाङ्गामल निष्कासित दुष्कार्यघ
निष्कावलि दीपप्रभ नीपऽच्छवि तापप्रद कनकमालिका
पिशङ्गितसर्वाङ्गः ॥ २३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
piṅgākṛtibhṛṅgārunibhāṅgāra dalāṅgāmala niṣkāsita duṣkāryagha
niṣkāvali dīpaprabha nīpa'cchavi tāpaprada kanakamālikā
piśaṅgitasarvāṅgaḥ .. 23 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Who is without any blemish; of immeasurable greatness; of true desire and true wisdom; the ocean of all auspicious qualities; the destroyer of all that is inauspicious.

contentBlocks.original:
नवदलित दलवलित मृदुललित कमलतति मदविहति चतुरतर
पृथुलतर सरसतर कनकसर मयरुचिर
कण्ठिका कमनीयकण्ठः ॥ २४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
navadalita dalavalita mṛdulalita kamalatati madavihati caturatara
pṛthulatara sarasatara kanakasara mayarucira
kaṇṭhikā kamanīyakaṇṭhaḥ .. 24 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Who is free from the influence of the three gunas; of self-luminous divine form; who is the inner controller of all; known only through the Vedas and Vedanta.

contentBlocks.original:
वाताशनाधिपतिशयन कमनपरिचरण रतिसमेताखिल फणधरतति
मतिकर कनकमय नागाभरण परिवीताखिलाङ्गावगमित शयन भूताहिराज
जातातिशयः ॥ २५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vātāśanādhipatiśayana kamanaparicaraṇa ratisametākhila phaṇadharatati
matikara kanakamaya nāgābharaṇa parivītākhilāṅgāvagamita śayana bhūtāhirāja
jātātiśayaḥ .. 25 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Whose divine form is an ocean of beauty and charm that captivates the minds and eyes of all — from Brahma and Shiva down to the blade of grass; who cannot be adequately described.

contentBlocks.original:
रविकोटी परिपाटी धरकोटिर वराटीकितवाटि रसधाटि धरमणिगण किरण
विसरण सततविधुत तिमिरमोहगर्भगेहः ॥ २६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ravikoṭī paripāṭī dharakoṭira varāṭīkitavāṭi rasadhāṭi dharamaṇigaṇa kiraṇa
visaraṇa satatavidhuta timiramohagarbhagehaḥ .. 26 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Who is the sole support of all the worlds; the refuge of all who have nowhere to go; the master of all; the inner soul of all beings.

contentBlocks.original: अपरिमित विविधभुवनभरिताखण्ड ब्रह्माण्डमण्डल पिचण्डिलः ॥ २७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: aparimita vividhabhuvanabharitākhaṇḍa brahmāṇḍamaṇḍala picaṇḍilaḥ .. 27 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Who is the treasure-house of infinite compassion, grace, affection, generosity and all auspicious qualities; who is the Lord of Sri (Lakshmi) herself; the protector and nourisher of all.

contentBlocks.original:
आर्यधुर्यानन्तार्य पवित्रखनित्रपात पात्रीकृत निजचुबुकगत व्रणकिण
विभूषणवहन सूचितश्रित जनवत्सलतातिशयः ॥ २८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āryadhuryānantārya pavitrakhanitrapāta pātrīkṛta nijacubukagata vraṇakiṇa
vibhūṣaṇavahana sūcitaśrita janavatsalatātiśayaḥ .. 28 ..
contentBlocks.translation: This is the Lord Sri Srinivasa — who is dear to Ramanuja and his tradition of Acharyas; who shines as the jewel crest of the Venkata hill; the supreme being adored by Brahma, Shiva and Indra.

contentBlocks.original:
मड्डुडिण्डिम डमरुझर्झर काहली पटहावली मृदुमर्दलालि मृदङ्ग
दुन्दुभि ढक्किकामुख हृद्यवाद्यक मधुरमङ्गल नादमेदुर विसृमर
सरसगान रसरुचिर सन्ततसन्तन्यमान नित्योत्सव पक्षोत्सव मासोत्सव
संवत्सरोत्सवादि विविधोत्सव कृतानन्दः ॥ २९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
maḍḍuḍiṇḍima ḍamarujharjhara kāhalī paṭahāvalī mṛdumardalāli mṛdaṅga
dundubhi ḍhakkikāmukha hṛdyavādyaka madhuramaṅgala nādamedura visṛmara
sarasagāna rasarucira santatasantanyamāna nityotsava pakṣotsava māsotsava
saṃvatsarotsavādi vividhotsava kṛtānandaḥ .. 29 ..
contentBlocks.translation: He is the sole means and the supreme goal; he is both the path and the destination; I am his servant in all my births; I seek refuge at his lotus feet.

contentBlocks.original: श्रीमदानन्दनिलयविमानवासः ॥ ३० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: śrīmadānandanilayavimānavāsaḥ .. 30 ..
contentBlocks.translation: O Lord, I have no other refuge; you are my father, mother, friend and guide; you alone are my protector and sustainer; O Venkatesha, be gracious to me.

contentBlocks.original: सततपद्मालयापदपद्मरेणुसञ्चित वक्षस्स्थलपटवासः ॥ ३१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: satatapadmālayāpadapadmareṇusañcita vakṣassthalapaṭavāsaḥ .. 31 ..
contentBlocks.translation: I surrender myself completely at your feet; free me from the ocean of samsara; grant me eternal service at your feet — this is all I desire, O Srinivasa.

contentBlocks.original: श्रीश्रीनिवासस्सुप्रसन्नो विजयताम् ॥ ३२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: śrīśrīnivāsassuprasanno vijayatām .. 32 ..
contentBlocks.translation: May I never forget you; may I always remember your lotus feet; may my mind be purified by constant devotion to you — O Srinivasa, be my eternal refuge.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरङ्गसूरिणेदं श्रीशैलानन्तसुरिवंश्येन ।
भक्त्या रचितं हृद्यम् गद्यम् गृह्णातु वेङ्कटेशानः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīraṅgasūriṇedaṃ śrīśailānantasurivaṃśyena .
bhaktyā racitaṃ hṛdyam gadyam gṛhṇātu veṅkaṭeśānaḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation: O Srinivasa, son of Vasudeva, dwelling on the beautiful Venkata hill together with Sri Padmavati — protect us always with your grace.

contentBlocks.original: ॥ श्री श्रीनिवासगद्यं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: .. śrī śrīnivāsagadyaṃ sampūrṇam ..
contentBlocks.translation: Thus the Sri Srinivasa Gadyam is complete.
